title: hieracium maculatum var. povedanum
intro: Hieracium maculatum var. povedanum, also known as Povedan's spotted hawkweed, is a rare and lesser-known variety of the Hieracium maculatum plant species, which belongs to the dandelion family (Asteraceae) and is native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve flatulence, as well as its use as a diuretic to help reduce water retention. The health benefits of Hieracium maculatum var. povedanum include its potential to help alleviate symptoms of indigestion, bloating, and other gastrointestinal issues, as well as its use as an anti-inflammatory agent. In herbal preparations, Hieracium maculatum var. povedanum is often made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which are then used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
